title: Exceptionally rare sighting of planets colliding may shed light on the crash that formed the moon
summary: Astronomers say a distant, sunlike star shows signs of a catastrophic planet-on-planet crash that may mirror the ancient impact that formed Earth's moon.

title: Children wearing bronze 'warrior' belts discovered in 2,500-year-old cemetery in Italy
summary: Archaeologists have uncovered the graves of two pre-Roman children who were buried like male warriors.

title: Scientists use 'negative light' to send secret messages hidden inside heat
summary: Using a phenomenon called "negative light," scientists invisibly transferred data disguised as background thermal radiation.

title: 'Interstellar messenger' 3I/ATLAS could be nearly as old as the universe itself, James Webb telescope observations reveal
summary: The comet formed in a cold and distant part of the early Milky Way up to 12 billion years ago, potentially putting it just under 2 billion years the age of the universe.

title: Bonobos are just as aggressive as chimps, but there's a key difference — the female bonobos
summary: A new study of chimpanzee and bonobo groups at zoos reveals similar levels of aggression. However, scientists found stark sex-based differences between the species.

title: Europe's oldest handgun may date to 14th-century siege at German castle
summary: The discovery of a handgun from 1390 found in Germany reveals that portable firearms were used earlier than thought in late medieval Europe.

title: Man in Czech Republic accidentally finds Bronze Age spearhead mold in his backyard
summary: A stone being used in the foundation of an old barn in the Czech Republic turned out to be a Bronze Age spearhead mold.

title: 'It's nature calling to humans, and humans deciding whether or not to reply': Why we need to start paying attention to our mutually beneficial relationships with other species
summary: Live Science spoke with Rob Dunn, an applied ecologist and author of the book "The Call of the Honeyguide," about "mutualism" — how different species team up for their mutual benefit — and how humans can feel more connected to nature.

title: Vernal equinox 2026: When is the first day of spring?
summary: The first day of spring 2026 in the Northern Hemisphere arrives with the equinox on March 20. Here's when and why the seasons change.

title: 1,300-pound spacecraft will crash to Earth today following intense solar activity, NASA warns
summary: NASA's Van Allen Probe A is falling to Earth much sooner than expected, though the spacecraft's reentry poses a low risk to humans.

title: Exotic prime numbers could be hiding inside black holes
summary: A new paper makes the strange case for prime numbers at the heart of physics.

title: California's wildfire season is shifting, with more blazes after the traditional high-risk window, study finds
summary: New research finds that climate-driven shifts in wildfire seasons in North America are different depending on the ecosystem.

title: Why aren't mammals as colorful as reptiles, birds or fish?
summary: Many mammals have fur the color of brown and black. Why don't they have more exotic colors, like purple and neon pink?

title: 'The warming trend nearly doubled after 2014': The rate of global warming has accelerated more in the past decade than ever before
summary: A new analysis finds that global warming has significantly accelerated since 2015, but not everyone agrees.

title: Why is mercury a liquid?
summary: Mercury is a metal, yet it has some weird physical properties, including being a liquid at room temperature.

title: Ancient 'alien-like' skulls have been found on every continent but Antarctica. Anthropologists are starting to figure out why.
summary: Humans have practiced head shaping for tens of thousands of years, and anthropologists are beginning to uncover clues as to why.

title: Planting trees in the sea could act as a huge carbon sink and save millions of dollars in storm damage every year. What is stopping us from doing it?
summary: A new study reveals restoring mangroves could save $800 million in storm damage, protect 140,000 people from flooding, and remove almost triple the amount of CO2 produced by cars in the U.S. every year.

title: Older women are winning more Oscars – here's why
summary: BBC research shows the average age of best actress nominees is rising

title: 10 ملايين دولار.. مكافأة أميركية لاعتقال شقيقين يقودان عصابة
summary: قالت وزارة الخارجية الأميركية يوم الخميس إنها ستدفع ما يصل إلى 10 ملايين دولار مقابل معلومات تؤدي إلى اعتقال أو إدانة شقيقين تم تحديدهما كزعيمين لعصابة سينالوا المكسيكية في ولاية باها كاليفورنيا، التي تضم مدينة تيخوانا.

title: تأثير إبستين يصل إلى "مؤتمر الديناصورات".. ماذا حدث؟
summary: قرر مؤتمر بريطاني متخصص في بحث تاريخ الديناصورات، منع حضور بعض علماء الحفريات المذكورين في ملفات جيفري إبستين، رجل الأعمال الأميركي المدان بانتهاكات جنسية.

title: في فيلم أميركي ضخم.. مشهد عن الأهرامات يثير غضب المصريين
summary: أثار مقطع من واحد من أشهر الأفلام الأميركية هذا العام، استياء عدد كبير من المصريين، بسبب معلومة تتعلق ببناء الأهرامات.
